title: Karaoke Kills — Demo Truth Pack
description: A reusable demo mystery pack showing timed Detective Mode clue unlocks.

answerKey.motive: The victim discovered Mara was rigging the contest and planned to expose her before the finale.
answerKey.explanation: The bank transfer, deleted social post, backstage map ping, and recovered audio note all point to Mara arranging the fatal stage setup before the final song.

publicClues.title: Case Opened
publicClues.text: Detectives are assigned to the venue after a performer collapses during the karaoke finale. The scene is secured and all evidence will be released into the shared investigation feed.

publicClues.title: Witness Pattern
publicClues.text: Several witnesses remember a staff member asking the victim to test the microphone shortly before the final performance. Nobody agrees on the exact wording.

publicClues.title: Timeline Tightens
publicClues.text: The stage was unattended for less than four minutes. The suspect must have known the backstage hallway and the contest schedule.

publicClues.title: Accusation Window Opening Soon
publicClues.text: Detectives should review the app evidence and prepare a final accusation when the accusation phase opens.

phone.title: Missed Call Log
phone.text: The victim missed three calls from Mara Voss between 7:42 PM and 7:49 PM. The calls stopped after the victim entered the backstage hallway.

phone.title: Deleted Voicemail Fragment
phone.text: A recovered fragment catches a tense voice saying, 'You were supposed to keep quiet until after tonight.'

messages.title: Contest Text Thread
messages.text: A message from the victim reads: 'If the scores are fake, the crowd deserves to know before the trophy goes out.'

messages.title: Unsent Draft
messages.text: The victim drafted a message naming Mara but never sent it. The draft says Mara controlled the score sheet and the stage order.

maps.title: Backstage Location Ping
maps.text: Mara's phone pinged near the backstage service door at 8:56 PM, two minutes before the microphone check.

bank.title: Unusual Transfer
bank.text: A transfer labeled 'finale cleanup' was sent from Mara's account to a temporary sound technician that afternoon.

photos.title: Stage Photo
photos.text: A fan photo shows the victim's microphone cable lying cleanly coiled at 8:37 PM. A later photo shows it routed under a loose floor mat.

social.title: Deleted Post Screenshot
social.text: A screenshot of Mara's deleted post says, 'No one ruins my finale twice.' It was posted thirty minutes before the collapse.

contacts.title: Suspect Statement: Owen Landry
contacts.text: Owen says the sponsor money was “already being corrected” and that Lena misunderstood the books. He sounds defensive, but he never admits to touching her drink.

contacts.title: Suspect Statement: Tessa Marin
contacts.text: Tessa says her public fight with Lena made her look guilty, but she argues that a loud argument is a terrible cover for a quiet crime.

contacts.title: Suspect Statement: Brielle Dupré
contacts.text: Brielle says she wanted the mystery night to trend. She admits she framed some shots dramatically, but says she did not stage the emergency.

contacts.title: Technician Contact
contacts.text: The temporary technician was saved in Mara's contacts under the nickname 'Wire Guy.'

contacts.title: Suspect Statement: Cole Vinet
contacts.text: Cole says he saw Owen near the office earlier, but he also admits the bar was crowded enough for almost anyone to disappear for a minute.

notes.title: Victim's Notebook
notes.text: The victim wrote, 'Mara has the wrong numbers. Ask for the original score cards before she announces the winner.'

files.title: Score Sheet Comparison
files.text: The official file and the printed score sheet disagree. Only Mara had edit access after 7:30 PM.

browser.title: Search History
browser.text: A browser search from Mara's phone asks how to make a cable fault look like an accident.
